dependencies: Centralized equipment purchases from key suppliers
Dependence on minimum number of suppliers per equipment category
Supply chain disruptions may impact ability to obtain equipment on acceptable terms
risks: Insolvency or financial difficulties of key suppliers
Supply chain disruptions impacting equipment availability
Inability to obtain equipment and supplies on acceptable terms or at all
Increased equipment costs due to commodity price pressures, inflation, and tariffs on raw materials

regulatory_risks: Environmental laws governing wastewater, storm water, solid and hazardous wastes and materials, and air quality
Liability for costs of investigating and remediating contamination at sites
Fines and penalties for environmental and safety non-compliance
Climate change regulation could increase compliance costs
Multiple state and local regulations across 49 U.S. states exposing to different requirements
Federal law and regulatory requirements as government contractor
Changes to income tax laws or regulations in U.S. and other jurisdictions
EU Pillar Two Directive establishing 15 percent minimum effective tax rate
Privacy and data protection laws including EU GDPR and U.S. state laws
Cyber incident reporting laws and regulations
Changes in laws affecting labor relations or operations
Compliance with foreign laws and regulations in Canada, Europe, Australia and New Zealand

sanction_risks: Geopolitical conflicts such as Russia's invasion of Ukraine and resultant sanctions
Sanctions and other measures imposed in response to geopolitical conflicts
